title: Quick check: the Cloud PBX list

questions.prompt: Customer reports 'calls aren't working and nothing has changed'. The Cloud PBX list shows their row with Status = 'Stopped'. What's your first conclusion?
choices: There's a SIP trunk problem; pull a trace.
The PBX has been explicitly stopped from YCM and won't carry calls until it's started; that's the cause, not a symptom.
questions.explanation: Only 'Running' carries calls. The other states (Stopped, Starting, Upgrading, Restoring, Provisioning) all mean calls are failing or disrupted. A Stopped PBX is the answer; you'd start it, or escalate if it was stopped deliberately by another team.

questions.prompt: If a customer's Cloud PBX row reads Extensions 50 / 50, attempting to create a 51st extension in PSE will fail until the PBX is resized in YCM.
questions.explanation: Capacity is a YCM-allocated limit. At cap, PSE will refuse the next extension. The fix lives in YCM (resize the Cloud PBX); knowing this saves a fruitless descent into PSE looking for a configuration knob that doesn't exist.
